query: SELECT vocabularyJson, version FROM platform_default_vocabulary ORDER BY version DESC LIMIT 1;
command: mysql --batch --raw --column-names --default-character-set=utf8mb4 --host gateway05.us-east-1.prod.aws.tidbcloud.com --port 4000 --user 2NoQxNASu25QqFj.3c7bc1be8a03 --database WKX2kfVZUM23yKDCoTqGRN --execute SELECT vocabularyJson, version FROM platform_default_vocabulary ORDER BY version DESC LIMIT 1;

rows.vocabularyJson: {"arcTerms": [{"instruction": "Movement is forward into the experience, not around or away from it", "term": "through not away from"}, {"instruction": "The image suggests "available light"}, {"instruction": "Every light source should be identifiable — where is it coming from, what is it", "term": "source traceable"}, {"instruction": "Avoid dramatic rim lights, colored gels, or lighting that announces itself as cinematic", "term": "no theatrical lighting"}, {"instruction": "Warm tones are present when the scene earns them — not applied as a filter over everything", "term": "warmth earned not imposed"}], "emotionalRegister": [{"instruction": "The image is happening now — not nostalgic, not anticipatory, but in the moment of the thing", "term": "present tense"}, {"instruction": "The emotional register is honest — not forced positivity, not performed darkness", "term": "neither triumphant nor defeated"}, {"instruction": "Represent the subject directly — not a symbol of the thing, not a metaphor for the thing, the thing", "term": "the thing itself"}], "environment": [{"instruction": "Light that has been through something — available light, source traceable, warmth that comes from somewhere real", "term": "earned light"}, {"instruction": "Surfaces and textures that are what they appear to be — no decorative fakery, no stock-image polish", "term": "honest materials"}, {"instruction": "The real world, not a stylized version of it — specific, particular, not generic", "term": "world as it actually is"}, {"instruction": "Empty space is intentional and "subject in context"}, {"instruction": "The subject is the size it actually is in the world — not heroically enlarged, not diminished", "term": "scale honest"}]}
